AccountDetail.f44f287.description: Full account view returned from POST/GET-by-id.
AccountDetail.f44f287.title: AccountDetail

AdapterConfigEnvelope.61b280c.FreeWheelA.description:
FreeWheel Publisher API adapter configuration.

Exactly one auth path is required: ``username`` + ``password`` (OAuth2
password grant — the canonical path, auto-refreshing) or ``api_token``
(pre-minted bearer, escape hatch for partner-provisioned tokens).
AdapterConfigEnvelope.61b280c.FreeWheelA.title: FreeWheelAdapterConfig

AdapterConfigEnvelope.61b280c.SpringServ.description:
SpringServe (Magnite) ad-server adapter configuration.

Exactly one auth path is required: ``email`` + ``password`` (the
canonical path -- the SpringServe API mints a 2-hour token from
these credentials and the adapter caches + refreshes) or
``api_token`` (a pre-minted token, escape hatch for partner-
provisioned tokens; no auto-refresh).
AdapterConfigEnvelope.61b280c.SpringServ.title: SpringServeAdapterConfig

AdapterConfigResponse.f44f287.description: Adapter config view returned with secrets redacted.
AdapterConfigResponse.f44f287.title: AdapterConfigResponse

ApiError.f44f287.description: Standard problem-detail shape used by every Tenant Management endpoint.
ApiError.f44f287.title: ApiError

ApproveWorkflowRequest.f44f287.description: ``POST /workflows/{wid}/approve`` body. Notes are optional on approve.
ApproveWorkflowRequest.f44f287.title: ApproveWorkflowRequest

BuyerAdvertiserMapping.f44f287.description:
Routing rule on the wire — the public face of an
``AdvertiserRoutingRule`` ORM row.

Vocabulary alignment with Scope3 Storefront UI: external surface uses
"buyer-advertiser-mapping"; internal storage table is named
``advertiser_routing_rules`` because the impl IS a precedence-ordered
routing chain. One-line mapping at the API boundary.
BuyerAdvertiserMapping.f44f287.title: BuyerAdvertiserMapping

CreateAccountRequest.f44f287.description:
Pre-map a GAM advertiser to a billing key.

Upserts by the same natural key ``_sync_accounts_impl`` uses so the
next ``sync_accounts`` call from a buyer agent finds the row already
wired and skips ``pending_provision`` entirely.

Validation:
- ``billing=agent`` requires ``buyer_agent_principal_id``.
- ``sandbox=True`` rejects ``gam_advertiser_id`` — sandbox routes to
  the per-tenant sandbox advertiser, not a caller-specified one.
CreateAccountRequest.f44f287.title: CreateAccountRequest

CreateAccountRequest.f44f287.BrandRef.description:
Compact brand reference. Mirrors AdCP ``BrandReference`` minimally —
``domain`` is the natural-key field; ``brand_id`` is an optional
publisher-side stable id when one brand owns multiple domains.
CreateAccountRequest.f44f287.BrandRef.title: BrandRef

CreateWebhookSubscriptionRequest.f44f287.description:
``POST /tenants/{tid}/webhooks`` body.

``event_types``: subset of :data:`WEBHOOK_EVENT_TYPES`; empty list means
"all events". ``secret`` may be omitted, in which case the server
generates one and returns it in the create response (exactly once).
CreateWebhookSubscriptionRequest.f44f287.title: CreateWebhookSubscriptionRequest

ListAccountsManagedResponse.f44f287.Acco.description: Compact account view used by list endpoints.
ListAccountsManagedResponse.f44f287.Acco.title: AccountSummary

ListAdaptersResponse.f44f287.description: Response from ``GET /adapters`` — full catalog of supported adapter types.
ListAdaptersResponse.f44f287.title: ListAdaptersResponse

ListAdaptersResponse.f44f287.AdapterCapa.description:
Operational capabilities flags surfaced on the discovery endpoint.

Mirrors ``src.adapters.base.AdapterCapabilities`` (the class-level
static on each adapter). Each adapter declares its caps; the discovery
endpoint just surfaces them to embedder clients so they can decide
which adapter to surface and which UI sections to render.
ListAdaptersResponse.f44f287.AdapterCapa.title: AdapterCapabilitiesSummary

ListAdaptersResponse.f44f287.AdapterCata.description: One supported adapter type returned by the discovery endpoint.

connection_schema.description: JSON Schema for this adapter's typed connection config (the discriminated union member).
connection_schema.title: Connection Schema

description.description: Short description of what this adapter does.
description.title: Description

tier.description: Whether this adapter is meant for production use. ``live`` = real ad-server integration intended for production pickers. ``test`` = simulated/dev-only adapter (Mock) — embedders should filter these out of production UI by default.
tier.title: Tier

ListAdaptersResponse.f44f287.AdapterCata.title: AdapterCatalogEntry

ListAuditLogResponse.f44f287.AuditLogEnt.description: One audit-log entry on the wire.
ListAuditLogResponse.f44f287.AuditLogEnt.title: AuditLogEntry

ListBuyerAdvertiserMappingsResponse.f44f.description:
Routing rule on the wire — the public face of an
``AdvertiserRoutingRule`` ORM row.

Vocabulary alignment with Scope3 Storefront UI: external surface uses
"buyer-advertiser-mapping"; internal storage table is named
``advertiser_routing_rules`` because the impl IS a precedence-ordered
routing chain. One-line mapping at the API boundary.
ListBuyerAdvertiserMappingsResponse.f44f.title: BuyerAdvertiserMapping

ListGamAdvertisersResponse.f44f287.description:
``GET /tenants/{tid}/gam/advertisers`` response.

Cursor pagination with opaque base64-encoded offset (same shape as
other list endpoints — Storefront treats it as a sealed token).

``synced_at`` reports the most-recent ``gam_advertisers.synced_at``
for the tenant so the picker can show "Last synced 5 minutes ago".
NULL when no advertisers have ever synced.
ListGamAdvertisersResponse.f44f287.title: ListGamAdvertisersResponse

ListGamAdvertisersResponse.f44f287.GamAd.description:
One row from the synced ``gam_advertisers`` cache, on the wire.

Source of truth is GAM's ``CompanyService.getCompaniesByStatement
WHERE type = 'ADVERTISER'``; this projection is what the Buyer
Routing UI's picker renders.
ListGamAdvertisersResponse.f44f287.GamAd.title: GamAdvertiser

ListMediaBuysResponse.f44f287.MediaBuySu.description:
Compact media-buy entry for ``GET /media-buys`` listings.

``pacing`` is computed (delivered ÷ expected-by-now); ``None`` when the
buy hasn't started yet or delivery data is unavailable.
ListMediaBuysResponse.f44f287.MediaBuySu.title: MediaBuySummary

ListRecentBuyersResponse.f44f287.RecentB.description:
One distinct ``(operator, brand_house, brand_id)`` triple seen in
recent traffic, with the GAM advertiser it resolved to and how.

Powers Storefront's "buyer routing" widget — publishers can see at a
glance which buyers are landing on the default advertiser
(``resolved_via=default``) and might want their own bucket, vs.
which already match a specific routing rule.
ListRecentBuyersResponse.f44f287.RecentB.title: RecentBuyer

ListSyncHistoryResponse.f44f287.SyncRunI.description: One sync-run entry in the historical timeline.
ListSyncHistoryResponse.f44f287.SyncRunI.title: SyncRunInfo

ListWebhooksResponse.f44f287.description:
``GET /tenants/{tid}/webhooks`` response.

Subscriptions are returned without secrets (use the create response if
you still have it; otherwise re-register).
ListWebhooksResponse.f44f287.title: ListWebhooksResponse

ListWebhooksResponse.f44f287.WebhookSubs.description: Subscription record without the secret. Used for list + get + delete responses.
ListWebhooksResponse.f44f287.WebhookSubs.title: WebhookSubscriptionSummary

ListWorkflowsResponse.f44f287.WorkflowSu.description:
Compact workflow entry for ``GET /workflows`` listings.

``subject_type``/``subject_id`` are the object the workflow is gating
(e.g. ``media_buy``/``mb_1234``). ``workflow_type`` is the human-readable
kind (``media_buy_approval``, ``creative_approval``, etc.) — sourced from
the WorkflowStep's ``tool_name`` falling back to ``step_type``.
ListWorkflowsResponse.f44f287.WorkflowSu.title: WorkflowSummary

MediaBuyDetail.f44f287.description:
Full media-buy detail including products, targeting, creatives, and
status history.
MediaBuyDetail.f44f287.title: MediaBuyDetail

MediaBuyDetail.f44f287.StatusEvent.description: One status-change entry in a media buy's history.
MediaBuyDetail.f44f287.StatusEvent.title: StatusEvent

PreviewAdapterRequest.f44f287.description: Pre-provision adapter probe — same union as :class:`ProvisionTenantRequest`.
PreviewAdapterRequest.f44f287.title: PreviewAdapterRequest

PreviewAdapterRequest.f44f287.FreeWheelA.description:
FreeWheel Publisher API adapter configuration.

Exactly one auth path is required: ``username`` + ``password`` (OAuth2
password grant — the canonical path, auto-refreshing) or ``api_token``
(pre-minted bearer, escape hatch for partner-provisioned tokens).
PreviewAdapterRequest.f44f287.FreeWheelA.title: FreeWheelAdapterConfig

PreviewAdapterRequest.f44f287.SpringServ.description:
SpringServe (Magnite) ad-server adapter configuration.

Exactly one auth path is required: ``email`` + ``password`` (the
canonical path -- the SpringServe API mints a 2-hour token from
these credentials and the adapter caches + refreshes) or
``api_token`` (a pre-minted token, escape hatch for partner-
provisioned tokens; no auto-refresh).
PreviewAdapterRequest.f44f287.SpringServ.title: SpringServeAdapterConfig

PreviewAdapterResponse.f44f287.description:
Adapter preview with network metadata for the Storefront UX.

``ok=False`` (bad creds) is returned with HTTP 200 — Storefront renders
this inline. Hard errors (malformed body, missing API key) still surface
via the normal 4xx path.
PreviewAdapterResponse.f44f287.title: PreviewAdapterResponse

ProvisionTenantRequest.f44f287.EmbedBrea.description:
First-crumb override for embedded-mode admin pages.

When the upstream host renders the salesagent admin UI inside its own
chrome (``tenant.is_embedded=True``), the first breadcrumb crumb should
point back to the host's storefront homepage rather than the
salesagent's tenant dashboard. ``label`` is the visible link text;
``url`` is the absolute HTTPS link the host wants the user dropped at.

Validated at schema boundary so bad input never reaches the rendered
template; the same model also gates the per-request
``X-Embed-Breadcrumb-Root`` header value (see
:func:`src.admin.utils.breadcrumbs.resolve_embed_breadcrumb_root`).
ProvisionTenantRequest.f44f287.EmbedBrea.title: EmbedBreadcrumbRoot

ProvisionTenantRequest.f44f287.FreeWheel.description:
FreeWheel Publisher API adapter configuration.

Exactly one auth path is required: ``username`` + ``password`` (OAuth2
password grant — the canonical path, auto-refreshing) or ``api_token``
(pre-minted bearer, escape hatch for partner-provisioned tokens).
ProvisionTenantRequest.f44f287.FreeWheel.title: FreeWheelAdapterConfig

ProvisionTenantRequest.f44f287.InitialPr.description: Optional initial advertiser created at provision time.
ProvisionTenantRequest.f44f287.InitialPr.title: InitialPrincipalRequest

ProvisionTenantRequest.f44f287.SpringSer.description:
SpringServe (Magnite) ad-server adapter configuration.

Exactly one auth path is required: ``email`` + ``password`` (the
canonical path -- the SpringServe API mints a 2-hour token from
these credentials and the adapter caches + refreshes) or
``api_token`` (a pre-minted token, escape hatch for partner-
provisioned tokens; no auto-refresh).
ProvisionTenantRequest.f44f287.SpringSer.title: SpringServeAdapterConfig

ProvisionTenantResponse.f44f287.AdapterS.description: Compact adapter status block returned in provision responses.
ProvisionTenantResponse.f44f287.AdapterS.title: AdapterStatusResponse

ProvisionTenantResponse.f44f287.InitialS.description:
Sync run ids spawned at provision time.

Storefront polls ``/status.syncs`` for progress per sync_type. Same
shape as ``RefreshResponse.sync_run_ids`` so callers can reuse the
refresh-poller path on first provision.
ProvisionTenantResponse.f44f287.InitialS.title: InitialSyncBlock

ProvisionTenantResponse.f44f287.Provisio.description:
Initial principal returned from provision.

Note: embedded-mode buyer-protocol auth flows through the identity-propagation
contract, not per-principal tokens (see sprint 2 § Auth boundary). No
``api_token`` field is emitted here.
ProvisionTenantResponse.f44f287.Provisio.title: ProvisionedPrincipalResponse

RefreshResponse.f44f287.description:
``POST /tenants/{tid}/refresh`` response — fan-out of sync run ids.

Storefront polls ``GET /status.syncs`` for progress per sync type.
Re-POST within 60 seconds returns the SAME ids (idempotent — avoids
hammering GAM when a publisher mashes the button).
RefreshResponse.f44f287.title: RefreshResponse

RejectWorkflowRequest.f44f287.description:
``POST /workflows/{wid}/reject`` body. Notes are required on reject —
every rejection has to carry a reason for the audit trail.
RejectWorkflowRequest.f44f287.title: RejectWorkflowRequest

TenantDetail.f44f287.description: Full tenant detail returned from ``GET /tenants/{id}`` and lifecycle responses.
TenantDetail.f44f287.title: TenantDetail

TenantDetail.f44f287.EmbedBreadcrumbRoot.description:
First-crumb override for embedded-mode admin pages.

When the upstream host renders the salesagent admin UI inside its own
chrome (``tenant.is_embedded=True``), the first breadcrumb crumb should
point back to the host's storefront homepage rather than the
salesagent's tenant dashboard. ``label`` is the visible link text;
``url`` is the absolute HTTPS link the host wants the user dropped at.

Validated at schema boundary so bad input never reaches the rendered
template; the same model also gates the per-request
``X-Embed-Breadcrumb-Root`` header value (see
:func:`src.admin.utils.breadcrumbs.resolve_embed_breadcrumb_root`).
TenantDetail.f44f287.EmbedBreadcrumbRoot.title: EmbedBreadcrumbRoot

TenantStatusResponse.f44f287.SetupTaskIt.description:
One configuration-completeness item in the status setup_tasks block.

Severity drives Storefront UI urgency; scope drives routing — Scope3
escalates ``platform`` items internally (it's the host's job to
finish provisioning), while ``publisher`` items deep-link into the
iframe at the matching Settings tab.
TenantStatusResponse.f44f287.SetupTaskIt.title: SetupTaskItem

TenantStatusResponse.f44f287.SetupTasksB.description:
Configuration-completeness rollup for ``GET /tenants/{tid}/status``.

Replaces the separate ``setup_checklist`` round-trip — Storefront
renders the homepage checklist directly off this block.
TenantStatusResponse.f44f287.SetupTasksB.title: SetupTasksBlock

TenantStatusResponse.f44f287.StatusAdapt.description: Adapter block of the tenant status snapshot.
TenantStatusResponse.f44f287.StatusAdapt.title: StatusAdapterBlock

TenantStatusResponse.f44f287.StatusMedia.description: Top-level media-buy counters (a buy contains 1+ packages).
TenantStatusResponse.f44f287.StatusMedia.title: StatusMediaBuysBlock

TenantStatusResponse.f44f287.StatusPacka.description:
Package-level counters (line items inside media buys).

``last_24h_impressions`` is set to 0 until delivery aggregation is wired
up — see :mod:`docs/design/embedded-mode-sprint-1.5.md` Open Q #3.
TenantStatusResponse.f44f287.StatusPacka.title: StatusPackagesBlock

TenantStatusResponse.f44f287.StatusProdu.description:
Product-level counters.

Distinct from :class:`StatusPackagesBlock` — one product can have
multiple priced packages, so package counts don't answer "what is
the publisher actually selling?". Storefront surfaces ``active_count``
on its homepage as the primary "what's the publisher doing" signal.

Note: the Product model doesn't carry an explicit ``status`` field
today. ``archived_at IS NULL`` rows count as active; non-null rows
count as archived. ``draft_count`` is 0 until a draft state lands
(forward-compatible field — Storefront can render a "Drafts" badge
without an API shape change when it does).
TenantStatusResponse.f44f287.StatusProdu.title: StatusProductsBlock

TenantStatusResponse.f44f287.StatusSyncs.description: Recent state of each sync category for a tenant.
TenantStatusResponse.f44f287.StatusSyncs.title: StatusSyncsBlock

TenantStatusResponse.f44f287.StatusWebho.description: Outbound-webhook summary. ``None`` until sprint 6 lands the table.
TenantStatusResponse.f44f287.StatusWebho.title: StatusWebhooksBlock

TestConnectionResponse.f44f287.description: Result of an adapter connection probe.
TestConnectionResponse.f44f287.title: TestConnectionResponse

UpdateBuyerAdvertiserMappingRequest.f44f.description:
``PATCH /tenants/{tid}/buyer-advertiser-mappings/{mid}`` body.

``operator_domain`` is intentionally absent — changing it requires
DELETE + POST so the natural-key uniqueness constraint can't be
silently violated by a partial-update flow.
UpdateBuyerAdvertiserMappingRequest.f44f.title: UpdateBuyerAdvertiserMappingRequest

UpdateTenantRequest.f44f287.description:
PATCH body — only platform-managed fields are exposed.

``external_org_id`` and ``external_source`` are NOT modifiable post-creation;
they identify the tenant's relationship with the upstream platform.
``is_active`` is changed via the deactivate/reactivate endpoints, not PATCH.
UpdateTenantRequest.f44f287.title: UpdateTenantRequest

UpdateTenantRequest.f44f287.EmbedBreadcr.description:
First-crumb override for embedded-mode admin pages.

When the upstream host renders the salesagent admin UI inside its own
chrome (``tenant.is_embedded=True``), the first breadcrumb crumb should
point back to the host's storefront homepage rather than the
salesagent's tenant dashboard. ``label`` is the visible link text;
``url`` is the absolute HTTPS link the host wants the user dropped at.

Validated at schema boundary so bad input never reaches the rendered
template; the same model also gates the per-request
``X-Embed-Breadcrumb-Root`` header value (see
:func:`src.admin.utils.breadcrumbs.resolve_embed_breadcrumb_root`).
UpdateTenantRequest.f44f287.EmbedBreadcr.title: EmbedBreadcrumbRoot

ValidationError.6a07bef.description: Model of a validation error response.
ValidationError.6a07bef.title: ValidationError

ValidationError.6a07bef.ValidationErrorE.description: Model of a validation error response element.
ValidationError.6a07bef.ValidationErrorE.title: ValidationErrorElement

WebhookSubscriptionCreatedResponse.f44f2.description:
Create-only response carrying the plaintext secret.

The ``secret`` field is returned exactly once at create time; subsequent
GETs omit it. Loss of the plaintext requires re-registering the webhook.
WebhookSubscriptionCreatedResponse.f44f2.title: WebhookSubscriptionCreatedResponse

WebhookSubscriptionSummary.f44f287.description: Subscription record without the secret. Used for list + get + delete responses.
WebhookSubscriptionSummary.f44f287.title: WebhookSubscriptionSummary

WebhookTestResponse.f44f287.description:
``POST /tenants/{tid}/webhooks/{wid}/test`` response.

Returns synthetic delivery results (one per registered event type) so
operators can verify the receiver works for every event they subscribe to.
WebhookTestResponse.f44f287.title: WebhookTestResponse

WebhookTestResponse.f44f287.WebhookTestD.description: One synthetic event delivery result included in :class:`WebhookTestResponse`.
WebhookTestResponse.f44f287.WebhookTestD.title: WebhookTestDeliveryResult

WorkflowDetail.f44f287.description:
Full workflow detail. Adds description, full request context, and
the decisions audit trail.
WorkflowDetail.f44f287.title: WorkflowDetail

WorkflowDetail.f44f287.WorkflowDecision.description: One approve/reject event recorded against a workflow.
WorkflowDetail.f44f287.WorkflowDecision.title: WorkflowDecision

get.description:
Embedder clients (Scope3 storefront, etc.) call this to discover what adapters this Sales Agent instance supports. Returns one entry per adapter type that has a typed AdapterConfig member — covering the full set surfaced to operators in the tenant settings UI.

Each entry carries:   - ``type`` — the value that goes into ``AdapterConfig.type``   - ``name`` / ``description`` — human-readable display strings   - ``tier`` — ``"live"`` for production adapters, ``"test"`` for     simulated/dev-only adapters (Mock). Embedders should filter     ``tier="test"`` out of production pickers.   - ``default_channels`` — channels this adapter is primarily used for   - ``capabilities`` — static AdapterCapabilities flags   - ``connection_schema`` — JSON Schema for the typed connection payload

Optional query params:   - ``tier=live`` to return only production-grade adapters (omit     Mock). Useful for production storefronts that should never offer     a simulated picker option.
get.summary: Return the full catalog of supported ad-server adapter types.
Health check endpoint for the tenant management API.
List tenants. Optional query params: ``is_embedded`` (or deprecated ``managed_externally``), ``is_active``, ``external_source``.

post.description: Lets the Storefront UI confirm an adapter grant + auto-fill currency and timezone before committing to a tenant. Bad creds return 200 with ``ok=false`` (renders inline) — only malformed bodies / missing API key surface as 4xx via the normal middleware path.
post.summary: Probe an adapter and return network metadata — no persistence.
Provision a managed tenant (one-shot create + adapter + currency + property tag + optional principal).

delete.description: Returns 409 ``tenant_has_active_resources`` if the tenant has any active media buys.
delete.summary: Soft-delete a tenant by default. Hard-delete requires ``?hard=true`` and ``X-Confirm-Delete: yes``.

get.summary: Return :class:`TenantDetail` for a tenant. 404 if the tenant doesn't exist.

patch.summary: Update platform-managed fields on a tenant (PATCH semantics — only listed fields are touched).

get.summary: List Accounts for a tenant. Filters: ``operator``, ``billing``, ``status``, ``sandbox``, ``advertiser_mapped``.

post.description: Upserts by the same natural key ``_sync_accounts_impl`` uses so a later ``sync_accounts`` call from a buyer agent finds the row already wired and skips the ``pending_provision`` round trip. Returns 201 on create, 200 on update.
post.summary: Pre-map a GAM advertiser to a billing key.

get.summary: Return the tenant's adapter config with secrets redacted.

put.summary: Replace the tenant's adapter config. Tests the connection before commit.

post.summary: Probe the saved adapter config without modifying state.

get.summary: List audit log entries for a tenant.

get.description: Filters: ``operator_domain`` (exact match) — the per-operator detail pane uses this to scope the rules grid without re-pulling the full set.
get.summary: List routing rules for a tenant. Ordered by ``created_at`` ASC so the UI renders them in the same order they were authored.

post.description:
Validation: - ``brand_id`` cannot be set without ``brand_house`` (sprint 1.8 doc §2:   a brand-level rule must be scoped to a parent house). - 409 on duplicate ``(operator_domain, brand_house, brand_id)`` tuple   (NULLs participate in uniqueness via COALESCE in the unique index).

Validation: ``gam_advertiser_id`` must reference a row in this tenant's synced ``gam_advertisers`` cache (Sprint 5 piece D — the deferred Sprint 1.8 validator finally lands here).

Graceful degradation: if the cache is empty (sync hasn't run yet — new tenant, GAM not connected, etc.) we skip the check and accept the id. This avoids breaking the rule-creation flow during onboarding before the first sync completes.

delete.description: Idempotency: DELETE on an already-deleted id returns 404 (not 204) — the caller asked us to delete a specific row by id, and a 404 is the truthful answer that the row isn't there. Callers driving a UI delete button should treat 404 as a benign race (someone else deleted it).
delete.summary: Delete a routing rule. 204 on success, 404 if not found.

patch.description: ``operator_domain`` is intentionally not patchable (see schema docstring — natural-key changes go DELETE+POST so collisions surface explicitly). Patching ``brand_house`` / ``brand_id`` can collide with another rule; 409 on natural-key conflict, same shape as POST.

post.summary: Idempotently deactivate a tenant. Calling on an already-inactive tenant is a no-op.

get.description:
Reads from the local cache, never from live GAM (10k+ advertiser networks make per-keystroke round trips prohibitive). Sync is triggered separately via ``POST /refresh`` or the cron worker.

Query params: - ``q`` (str, optional) — case-insensitive substring on ``name`` OR   exact match on ``id`` if numeric. ``q`` < 2 chars returns the   first page unfiltered (avoids expensive scan from typing first   character). - ``limit`` (int, default 50, max 500) — page size. - ``cursor`` (opaque base64, optional) — page bookmark.

``synced_at`` reports the most-recent ``gam_advertisers.synced_at`` for the tenant so the picker can show "Last synced 5 minutes ago".
get.summary: Searchable, paginated read over the synced ``gam_advertisers`` cache.

get.description: Query params: ``status``, ``principal_id``, ``from_date``, ``to_date``, ``limit``, ``cursor``. Date filters apply to ``flight_start_date``.
get.summary: List media buys for a tenant.

get.description:
Source data: ``Account`` rows joined to ``MediaBuy`` for activity counts. Each Account already carries its (operator, brand) natural key + the resolved ``platform_mappings.google_ad_manager.advertiser_id`` + ``resolved_via`` (sprint 1.8 stamp).

Query params: - ``days`` (int, default 30, max 365) — window for last_seen_at filter - ``limit`` (int, default 100, max 1000) — paginate by ordered last_seen_at desc

Returns ``{"buyers": [...]}``. Empty buyers list is the "no recent activity" case — never 404 unless the tenant itself doesn't exist.
get.summary: Distinct (operator, brand_house, brand_id) triples seen recently.

post.description:
For each enabled sync type, either reuse the existing SyncJob if one started in the last 60 seconds (or is currently running), or create a new pending SyncJob. The actual sync work is picked up by the existing background sync infrastructure.

Returns 202 Accepted with ``sync_run_ids`` mapping sync_type → sync_id. Storefront polls ``GET /status.syncs`` for per-type progress.
post.summary: Fan out a refresh across all sync types — collapses N per-sync triggers into one call.

get.description: One round-trip, one cache lifetime — covers adapter health, sync runs, open workflows, media-buy/package counters, and creative state. The response is computed (not stored) and cached in-memory for ~5s.
get.summary: Consolidated operational snapshot for a tenant.

get.description:
Query params: ``sync_type`` (``inventory`` / ``custom_targeting`` / ``advertisers``), ``status``, ``limit`` (default 20, max 500), ``cursor``. Default sort: ``started_at desc``.

Current sync state is in ``GET /tenants/{tid}/status`` — this endpoint is the timeline drill-down.
get.summary: List historical sync runs for a tenant.

get.description: Secrets are NEVER returned — they were surfaced exactly once at create time. To rotate, delete the subscription and create a new one.
get.summary: List active webhook subscriptions for a tenant.

post.description: Returns the plaintext ``secret`` exactly once (in this response). It is not retrievable later — the caller MUST persist it. Lost secrets require re-registering. Receivers verify HMAC-SHA256 signatures using the secret.
post.summary: Register a new outbound webhook subscription.

delete.description: Sets ``is_active=false`` so the row stays around for audit-log references but the publisher stops dispatching. The plaintext secret is dropped from the in-process cache so future re-registrations don't accidentally reuse it.

get.summary: Return a single subscription record. Secret is omitted.

post.description:
Returns one delivery result per registered event type. ``delivered`` on the response is the AND of all per-event ``delivered`` flags. Used by host products to verify the receiver is wired up correctly.

Failures here do NOT auto-disable the subscription — the consecutive- failures counter is incremented just like a real delivery, so flapping test runs eventually trip the disablement threshold.
post.summary: Synchronously fire a synthetic event of every registered type.

get.description: Query params: - ``status`` (repeatable): filter by wire-side status. Multiple values   OR together. Defaults to all statuses. - ``workflow_type``: exact match against ``tool_name`` or ``step_type``. - ``limit`` (int, default 50, max 500) - ``cursor`` (opaque base64): bookmark from a previous response.
get.summary: List workflow steps for a tenant, sorted with pending first.

post.summary: Approve a workflow. Idempotent: re-approving returns 200 with the existing state. Conflicting re-decide (approve after reject) returns 409. Expired workflows can't be approved.
Reject a workflow. Notes are required. Idempotent re-rejection returns 200 with existing state; conflicting decision returns 409.
